REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT ALBERT WONG, JOYCE WONG DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO NOVEMBER 9, 2020 FOR PLAINTIFF TO PROVIDE SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEFING ADDRESSING ANY AUTHORITY STANDING FOR THE PROPOSITION THAT "BY VIRTUE OF POSSESSION OF THE NOTE, ALL INTEREST IN BOTH THE NOTE AND THE CORRESPONDING DEED OF TRUST HAS BEEN TRANSFERRED TO PLAINTIFF." PLAINTIFF SHALL FURTHER IDENTIFY IN THE BRIEF THE DATE OF PURPORTED TRANSFER OF THE INTEREST IN THE DEED OF TRUST REFERENCED IN PARAGRAPH 12 OF THE COMPLAINT. (NOTE: PLAINTIFF SHALL PROVIDE INFORMATION REGARDING TRANSFER, NOT REGARDING THE RECORDING OF SUCH ASSIGNMENT. COMPLAINT IS CLEAR THAT RECORDING NEVER OCCURRED). IF SUCH DATE IS CORRESPONDING WITH THE DATE OF NOTE TRANSFER (APRIL 5, 2007 PER EX. 2 TO COMPLAINT) PLAINTIFF SHALL ALSO CITE AUTHORITY FOR THE PROPOSITION THAT THE "THE TIME TO SEEK THIS EQUITABLE RELIEF WOULD NOT EXPIRE AS LONG AS THE DEED OF TRUST IS STILL ENFORCEABLE." (OPPOSITION 3:14-16). FINALLY, PLAINTIFF SHALL ADDRESS AUTHORITIES SUPPORTING PROPRIETY OF NAMING MOVING DEFENDANTS AS A PARTY TO THE EQUITABLE ASSIGNMENT CAUSE OF ACTION IN LIGHT OF PLAINTIFF'S ARGUMENT IN OPPOSITION AT 3:1-5. BRIEF SHALL NOT EXCEED 3 PAGES; SHALL ONLY ADDRESS THE ISSUES LISTED ABOVE; AND SHALL NOT INCLUDE ARGUMENT BEYOND A BRIEF EXPLANATION OF WHY AUTHORITIES STAND FOR THE PROPOSITIONS FOR WHICH THEY ARE CITED. JUDGE CHARLES F. HAINES, CLERK: N. WONG, NOT REPORTED (D501)
MINI MINUTES FOR NOV-09-2020 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 501

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-09-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via CourtCall:
Ruby Chavez, Esq., 714-848-7920, appearing for Bank of New York, Plaintiff
No other appearances.

Hearing held. Ms. Chavez stated that she does not contest to the tentative ruling.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS ALBERT WONG, JOYCE WONG'S DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to amend the complaint in accordance with its supplemental brief sections II and IV. The Court does not reach the issue of the Statute of Limitations at this time. Such issue may be raised if necessary and proper upon the filing of the amended complaint. Plaintiff shall have 30 days from notice of entry of order to file its amended complaint.
Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC3.1312(b). Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk: N. Wong, Not reported (D501)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-09-2020 Continued To Nov-09-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Courtcall:
David Martin, Esq., 559-827-2948, appearing for Angela Stidham, Defendants
No other appearances.

Hearing held. Mr. Martin stated that he does not contest to the tentative ruling.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ALBERT WONG, JOYCE WONG DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is continued to November 9, 2020 for Plaintiff to provide supplemental briefing addressing any authority standing for the proposition that "by virtue of possession of the Note, all interest in both the Note and the corresponding deed of trust has been transferred to Plaintiff." Plaintiff shall further identify in the brief the date of purported transfer of the interest in the Deed of Trust referenced in paragraph 12 of the Complaint. (NOTE: Plaintiff shall provide information regarding transfer, not regarding the recording of such assignment. Complaint is clear that recording never occurred). If such date is corresponding with the date of Note transfer (April 5, 2007 per Ex. 2 to Complaint) Plaintiff shall also cite authority for the proposition that the "the time to seek this equitable relief would not expire as long as the deed of trust is still enforceable." (Opposition 3:14-16). Finally, Plaintiff shall address authorities supporting propriety of naming moving defendants as a party to the equitable assignment cause of action in light of Plaintiff's argument in opposition at 3:1-5. Brief shall not exceed 3 pages; shall only address the issues listed above; and shall not include argument beyond a brief explanation of why authorities stand for the propositions for which they are cited. Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk: N. Wong, Not reported (D501)
